refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the Vehicle to complete the load→travel→dump cycle with a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement clarifies that cycle time is a measurable outcome dependent on vehicle performance, operational conditions, and safety constraints. The p95 metric and duty cycle context are essential to avoid over-specification or under-specification of performance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle conditions; telemetry capture of cycle time distribution; statistical analysis of p95 percentile; comparison against CT_target baseline.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The Vehicle shall execute load operations within the cycle time budget allocated for loading.
FR-2.2: The Vehicle shall execute travel operations (loaded and empty) within the cycle time budget allocated for transit.
FR-2.3: The Vehicle shall execute dump operations within the cycle time budget allocated for dumping.
FR-2.4: The Safety Services shall enforce operational constraints that do not degrade cycle time below acceptable thresholds.
FR-2.5: The Teleoperation system shall provide responsive control inputs to the Vehicle with latency ≤ acceptable_latency_threshold.

candidate_dps: DP-2.A: Optimize vehicle powertrain (engine, transmission, hydraulics) for peak efficiency and responsiveness across the duty cycle; tune load/travel/dump sequences for minimal dwell time.
DP-2.B: Implement predictive cycle time monitoring via telemetry; use real-time feedback to alert operator and safety system of cycle time drift; enable adaptive operational modes.
DP-2.C: Design safety constraints (speed governors, load limits, interlock logic) to permit maximum safe performance without unnecessary cycle time penalties.
DP-2.D: Establish teleoperation network architecture with low-latency command/feedback loop to minimize operator reaction time and remote control overhead.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2: Integrated approach combining vehicle powertrain optimization (DP-2.A), real-time cycle time monitoring and adaptive feedback (DP-2.B), safety-aware performance tuning (DP-2.C), and low-latency teleoperation architecture (DP-2.D) to achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target.

potential_sources: Safety constraints (DP-2.C) may conflict with powertrain optimization (DP-2.A) if governors are too conservative; mitigation: co-design safety and performance parameters.
Teleoperation latency (DP-2.D) depends on network infrastructure outside NGHE control; mitigation: specify maximum acceptable latency and network SLA.
Cycle time monitoring (DP-2.B) requires accurate telemetry; coupling to vehicle instrumentation (DP-2.A) must be verified.
coupling_check.notes: The four sub-DPs are largely independent in their realization but coupled in their performance outcomes. Decoupling is achieved by establishing clear performance budgets (cycle time allocation, latency budget, safety margins) and interface contracts.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within the physical constraints of the vehicle and site conditions; Risk: if CT_target is unrealistic, no DP can satisfy it.
Assumption: Duty cycle definition is stable and representative; Risk: actual field conditions may differ, invalidating cycle time predictions.
Assumption: Teleoperation network infrastructure meets latency SLA; Risk: network congestion or outages degrade remote control responsiveness.
Assumption: Safety constraints can be tuned without eliminating cycle time headroom; Risk: over-conservative safety logic may make CT_target unachievable.
Assumption: Operator skill and worksite logistics do not dominate cycle time; Risk: human factors and site layout may be the limiting factor, not vehicle performance.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Decompose DP-2 into four separate DPs (DP-2.A, DP-2.B, DP-2.C, DP-2.D) at the next level to enable independent design and verification of powertrain, monitoring, safety, and teleoperation subsystems. Establish explicit performance budgets and interface contracts to manage coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: The Vehicle module encompasses the physical powertrain (engine, transmission, hydraulics), load/travel/dump actuation systems, onboard instrumentation and telemetry sensors, and embedded control logic for cycle time optimization and safety constraint enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle transmits real-time cycle time metrics, powertrain state, load status, and performance telemetry to Teleoperation and Safety Services for monitoring and adaptive control.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives operational commands (load, travel, dump) from Teleoperation or autonomous control logic; commands include target parameters (load mass, travel distance, dump height).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives safety constraints and emergency stop signals from Safety Services; enforces speed governors, load limits, and operational interlocks.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle communicates with Teleoperation and Safety Services via network; supports command/feedback loop with latency ≤ acceptable_latency_threshold.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level container; cycle time KPI is a system-level outcome, not a localized function. Assigning IM to NGHE Solution would diffuse responsibility across all subsystems without clear ownership. Not recommended as primary IM.

Vehicle is the primary realization of cycle time performance. Powertrain optimization, load/travel/dump actuation, onboard instrumentation, and embedded control logic are all Vehicle responsibilities. Cycle time monitoring and safety constraint enforcement are Vehicle functions. High containment, low diffusion. Recommended primary IM.

Teleoperation contributes to cycle time via operator responsiveness and command latency, but does not directly control vehicle performance. Teleoperation is a secondary contributor (DP-2.D). Assigning primary IM to Teleoperation would obscure vehicle-level performance ownership. Not recommended as primary IM.

Safety Services enforces constraints that affect cycle time (DP-2.C), but does not directly optimize cycle time. Safety Services is a constraint provider, not a performance optimizer. Assigning primary IM to Safety Services would misalign ownership. Not recommended as primary IM.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Vehicle module is the primary realization of cycle time KPI. It contains the powertrain, actuation systems, instrumentation, and embedded control logic necessary to achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target. Teleoperation and Safety Services are external interfaces that provide commands and constraints; they do not own cycle time performance.
containment_risks: Risk: Cycle time performance depends on site conditions (terrain, load characteristics, operator skill) that are outside Vehicle control. Mitigation: define duty cycle and site conditions as part of acceptance criteria; establish baseline performance under controlled conditions.

Risk: Teleoperation latency and operator responsiveness may dominate cycle time in remote operation mode. Mitigation: specify maximum acceptable latency and operator reaction time; allocate cycle time budget to teleoperation overhead.

Risk: Safety constraints may be over-conservative, limiting Vehicle performance. Mitigation: co-design safety and performance parameters; establish safety margins that do not eliminate cycle time headroom.

Risk: Vehicle instrumentation and telemetry may be incomplete, preventing accurate cycle time monitoring. Mitigation: specify required sensors and data quality; validate telemetry accuracy in field trials.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that Vehicle module includes all necessary instrumentation (cycle time sensors, powertrain state sensors, load sensors) and embedded control logic (cycle time optimization, safety constraint enforcement). Establish clear interface contracts with Teleoperation (Tasking, Network Link) and Safety Services (SafetyAlert) to define performance budgets and latency requirements.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-2 (integrated powertrain optimization, cycle time monitoring, safety-aware tuning, and low-latency teleoperation) directly addresses FR-2 (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target). The DP is comprehensive and covers all major performance drivers. No independence violations detected; the four sub-DPs are largely decoupled via performance budgets and interface contracts.

diffusion_risks: Cycle time monitoring (DP-2.B) requires telemetry infrastructure that spans Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Mitigation: establish Telemetry interface contract; assign primary ownership to Vehicle for onboard instrumentation and data collection.

Safety constraint enforcement (DP-2.C) requires coordination between Vehicle (embedded control logic) and Safety Services (constraint provider). Mitigation: establish SafetyAlert interface contract; define clear responsibility boundaries.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module contains the primary realization of DP-2 (powertrain, actuation, instrumentation, embedded control). Teleoperation and Safety Services are external interfaces that provide inputs (Tasking, SafetyAlert) and receive outputs (Telemetry). Containment is high; diffusion is managed via interface contracts.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via telemetry and field trials. Verification approach: instrument Vehicle with cycle time sensors; capture telemetry during representative duty cycle; analyze p95 percentile; compare against CT_target. Feasibility is high.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Vehicle is the primary owner of cycle time performance. Teleoperation and Safety Services are secondary contributors via interface contracts. Allocation is clear and unambiguous.

recommended_revisions: Establish explicit performance budgets: allocate cycle time to load, travel, and dump phases; allocate latency budget to teleoperation; allocate safety margin to constraint enforcement.
Define duty cycle and site conditions as part of acceptance criteria; specify baseline performance under controlled conditions.
Specify required Vehicle instrumentation (cycle time sensors, powertrain state sensors, load sensors) and telemetry data quality requirements.
Establish interface contracts for Telemetry (data format, frequency, accuracy), Tasking (command format, latency), SafetyAlert (constraint format, enforcement logic), and Network Link (latency SLA).

decision.justification: FR-2 is not atomic; it decomposes into five sub-FRs (load, travel, dump, safety, teleoperation). DP-2 is a composite solution that requires further decomposition into four sub-DPs. Vehicle is the correct IM boundary for the primary realization, but the next level must decompose the sub-FRs and sub-DPs to enable detailed design and verification. Proceed to the next decomposition level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The Vehicle shall execute load operations within the cycle time budget allocated for loading.
FR-2.2: The Vehicle shall execute travel operations (loaded and empty) within the cycle time budget allocated for transit.
FR-2.3: The Vehicle shall execute dump operations within the cycle time budget allocated for dumping.
FR-2.4: The Safety Services shall enforce operational constraints that do not degrade cycle time below acceptable thresholds.
FR-2.5: The Teleoperation system shall provide responsive control inputs to the Vehicle with latency ≤ acceptable_latency_threshold.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within the physical constraints of the vehicle and site conditions.
Duty cycle definition is stable and representative of actual field conditions.
Teleoperation network infrastructure meets latency SLA.
Safety constraints can be tuned without eliminating cycle time headroom.
Operator skill and worksite logistics do not dominate cycle time; vehicle performance is the limiting factor.
Vehicle instrumentation and telemetry are complete and accurate.
Site conditions (terrain, load characteristics) are within the design envelope.
open_risks: CT_target may be unrealistic; field validation required.
Actual duty cycle may differ from design assumptions; adaptive tuning may be necessary.
Teleoperation latency may exceed SLA due to network congestion or infrastructure limitations.
Safety constraints may be over-conservative, limiting vehicle performance.
Human factors (operator skill, worksite logistics) may dominate cycle time.
Vehicle instrumentation may be incomplete or inaccurate; telemetry quality must be validated.
evidence_needed: Duty cycle definition and site condition specifications.
Teleoperation network architecture and latency SLA.
Safety constraint specifications and performance margins.
Vehicle instrumentation specifications and telemetry data quality requirements.
Field trial results validating p95 cycle time performance.
Operator feedback on responsiveness and control latency.
